Welcome!

We are the Data Science & Artificial Intelligence Lab (DSAIL) at KAIST, led by Prof. Chanyoung Park.

Our research aims to push the frontier of AI by developing foundation models and agentic systems that can understand, reason, and act over complex, structured, and multimodal data. We are particularly interested in learning from relational data such as graphs, molecules, and dynamic systems, and enabling AI to move beyond prediction toward reasoning, decision-making, and scientific discovery.

We tackle fundamental challenges along three key dimensions:

  1. Representation: Learning expressive and unified representations from heterogeneous data sources—including graphs, molecules, sequences, and multimodal signals—while capturing their underlying relational structures.
  2. Reasoning: Enabling AI systems to perform compositional and cross-modal reasoning over structured and multimodal data.
  3. Decision-Making: Developing agentic AI systems that can plan, optimize, and interact with environments to solve complex real-world and scientific problems.

Research Areas

AI Foundations
• Foundation Models for Structured & Relational Data (Graph, Molecular, Scientific ML)
• Multimodal Foundation Models (Text, Graph, Sequence, Spectra, Vision)
• Large Language Models & Scientific LLMs (Reasoning, Tool Use)
• Agentic AI & Decision-Making Systems (LLM Agents, Planning, Multi-agent Systems)
• Generative Models & Inverse Design (Diffusion, Optimization)
• Trustworthy & Interpretable AI (Explainability, Robustness, Reliability)
• Continual & Adaptive Learning (Lifelong Learning, Distribution Shift)
• Causal & Data-centric AI 		Applications
• AI for Scientific Discovery (Biology, Chemistry, Materials)
• Molecular Design & Drug Discovery (Protein–Ligand Interaction, Generative Design)
• Autonomous Scientific Agents (AI-driven Experimentation & Discovery)
• Recommender Systems & Personalization
• Graph-based Real-world Intelligence (Social Networks, Knowledge Graphs)
• Fraud Detection & Anomaly Detection
• Time-series & Spatio-temporal Modeling
• Healthcare & Biomedical AI (Single-cell, Clinical Data)
• Industrial & Manufacturing AI (Materials & Process Optimization)
• Multimodal Understanding & Scene Reasoning

Interested?

If you’re interested in joining our lab, send an email with your interests, CV, and transcript to cy.park (at) kaist.ac.kr.

News

March 2026

Six papers got accepted at ICLR 2026 Workshop (1 x AI and Partial Differential Equations (Oral), 3 x AI4Mat, 1 x AI with Recursive Self-Improvement, 1 x Generative and Experimental Perspectives for Biomolecular Design).

February 2026

Namkyeong Lee received the KAIST Presidential Best Ph.D. Thesis Award for his dissertation “Understanding Science through the Lens of AI.”

January 2026

Three papers got accepted at ICLR 2026.

January 2026

A paper got accepted at SSAC 2026 (MIT Sloan Sports Analytics Conference).

January 2026

A paper got accepted at WWW 2026.

December 2025

Nine undergraduate interns joined our lab.

December 2025

Prof. Park received the 2025 KAIST College of Engineering Impact Research Award.

November 2025

Sangwoo Seo started as a visiting Ph.D student at Yale University, USA, hosted by Prof. Rex Ying

November 2025

A paper got accepted at AAAI 2026.
